name: Four Seasons Resort Mauritius at Anahita

content.verdict: The Four Seasons Anahita is the best-integrated overwater villa resort in Mauritius — set on a private island connected by wooden boardwalks within the Beau Champ lagoon on the east coast, with overwater villas that offer genuine Indian Ocean overwater living at a standard only Four Seasons can deliver. The combination of overwater villas, an award-winning championship golf course (designed by Ernie Els), the Four Seasons Spa, and the resort's private island feel creates one of the most complete luxury resort experiences in the Indian Ocean.
content.best_room: The Two-Bedroom Water Villa is the ultimate honeymoon overwater option — direct water access from your private deck, glass floor panels above the lagoon, and Four Seasons service delivered to your private pier. The One-Bedroom Water Villas are also superb and more appropriate for just two guests. For couples who prefer land, the Beach Villas on the resort's private beaches are among the finest beach villas in Mauritius.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Island Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from the airport and board the resort's boat for a short water transfer to the private island — the arrival across the Beau Champ lagoon with the overwater villas appearing through the mangroves is one of the great Indian Ocean resort arrivals. Your butler is waiting at your villa pier with Champagne and the Four Seasons welcome that the brand has made into an art form. First dinner at the main restaurant's lagoon terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Overwater Morning & Lagoon Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Wake up on your overwater deck to absolute silence broken only by the soft lapping of the lagoon below. Breakfast served on your private deck, then a morning of snorkeling directly off the villa into the warm Mauritius lagoon. The Four Seasons Spa opens at 8am — book a Creole healing treatment before lunch, and spend the afternoon in the overwater hammock or your private pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dolphin Encounter & West Coast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrange the resort's famous dolphin snorkeling excursion — Mauritius's west coast is one of the few places in the world where spinner dolphins gather in large pods every morning, and swimming alongside them in the open ocean is extraordinary. The drive to the west coast (about 1 hour) passes through the island's most scenic sugar cane landscapes.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Four Seasons Spa & Golf Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full day at the Four Seasons Spa — Mauritius-specific treatments using local tropical plant extracts, traditional Creole massage techniques, and the spa's signature Anahita ritual treatment. In the afternoon, take a sunset golf cart tour of the Ernie Els championship course even if you don't play — the views from the course over the lagoon at golden hour are remarkable.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Île aux Cerfs & Snorkeling Safari
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat to the famous Île aux Cerfs for the day. The Four Seasons can arrange a private snorkeling safari with a marine biologist guide — following the reef from the island around to the outer coral garden where the diversity of marine life is exceptional. Return to Anahita for the evening and a special butler-arranged private dinner on the villa pier under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mauritius Cultural Immersion
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hire a driver through the concierge for a full Mauritius cultural day. Visit the Eureka Colonial House in Moka for an insight into 19th-century plantation life, continue to Port Louis's vibrant central market, and finish at the stunning Pamplemousses Botanical Garden — established in 1770, it's one of the finest tropical gardens in the world with its famous giant Victoria amazonica water lilies.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final dawn on the overwater deck as the lagoon turns from silver to blue below you. Breakfast in bed, one last swim off the villa pier, and the gentle pace of a Four Seasons departure. The boat transfer back to the mainland and the drive to the airport passes through a Mauritius that feels familiar now — the coconut palms, the lagoon colors, the sugar cane fields. You will be planning your return before you land.

caveats: The east coast location means the resort is affected by trade winds June-August, which can make the lagoon choppy on some days — the overwater villas' protected lagoon position mitigates this, but ocean-facing activities may be limited during windy periods.
Not adults-only — Four Seasons Anahita has a comprehensive kids club and is popular with families. The overwater villa section provides separation, but the main pool and restaurant areas are mixed.
The golf course adds significant appeal for golf-playing couples but occupies much of the resort's land — couples who don't play golf may find the resort slightly large and spread out.

faqs.question: Is Four Seasons Anahita good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It is one of the finest honeymoon resorts in the Indian Ocean — the overwater villas deliver a genuine Maldivian-quality overwater experience within Mauritius, and the Four Seasons service standard is consistently exceptional. The private island setting, multiple restaurants, and the spa create a comprehensive luxury experience that leaves nothing wanting.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May through November (the Mauritius dry season) is best, with October-November offering the warmest and calmest conditions before the wet season. June-August can be breezy on the east coast, though the lagoon's sheltered position makes this less of an issue than at unprotected east coast resorts.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Water Villas book out quickly — reserve as early as possible, specifying a One-Bedroom Water Villa. Arrange the dolphin snorkeling excursion well in advance as it's weather-dependent and books up. The spa's featured treatments and the private pier dinner should be arranged through your butler immediately upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Four Seasons Anahita is not adults-only. It has a comprehensive kids club and welcomes families. The resort's size and layout mean families and couples occupy different spaces effectively, but couples seeking a fully adults-only environment should consider other properties.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A One-Bedroom Water Villa is the ideal honeymoon accommodation — private deck over the lagoon, glass floor panels, direct water entry, and butler service to your pier. If overwater is unavailable, a Beach Villa with private pool and direct beach access is the best land-based alternative.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Mauritius (MRU). The resort is on the east coast, approximately 1 hour by private car from the airport. From the reception building, a short boat ride transfers you to the private island. The hotel manages all arrival logistics seamlessly, including airport transfers and the boat transfer to your villa.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Four Seasons Resort Mauritius at Anahita

Dear Four Seasons Anahita team,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon and are very interested in staying with you from [DATE] to [DATE] ([X] nights).

We are particularly interested in: a One-Bedroom Water Villa

Could you please let us know:
- Current availability for our dates
- Any honeymoon packages or complimentary amenities
- Dolphin snorkeling excursion booking and private pier dinner arrangements

Thank you for your time.

[Your names]
